reasoning: The user explicitly requested to pick up the cup 'with anger' and also mentioned 'hot cup'. This combines into an 'angry_hot' emotion. To show anger, I will use fast, forceful, and jerky movements. To show 'hot', I will have the robot quickly drop the cup after picking it up and then react as if burned. I will use anticipation by having the arm jerk aggressively before moving. Timing will be used with wait() calls for dramatic pauses: before the angry grab, a freeze after picking up (realization of heat), and after dropping. Exaggeration will be applied through extreme speed and violent shaking. The follow-through will be a final, exaggerated shaking of the arm in pain/anger.

steps.description: Detect and confirm the cup position on the table with an aggressive, quick scan.
Anticipation: Jerk the arm aggressively and impatiently, showing building anger before the move.
Timing: A tense, brief pause, letting the anger simmer before the action.
Slam the arm down forcefully and quickly towards the cup, showing aggression and impatience.
Grab the cup with an aggressive, fast motion, then immediately recoil slightly as if burned by the hot object.
Timing: Freeze! A brief, sharp moment of shock and realization that the cup is burning hot.
Exaggeration: Violently jerk the arm away from the table, trying to get rid of the hot object in a panic.
Release the cup abruptly and forcefully, as if throwing it away due to the heat.
Timing: A moment of relief/shock after dropping the hot object. Hold still briefly.
Follow Through: Shake the arm violently and rapidly, as if trying to cool off a burned hand, expressing both anger at the situation and pain from the heat.
